message:
fix(database): cap SCRAM iterations to prevent DoS, flush TLS writes, eagerly rollback dropped transactions

PostgreSQL (postgres.rs):
- Cap SCRAM PBKDF2 iteration count at 600,000. A malicious server could
  previously send an arbitrarily high iteration count (e.g., 2^31),
  causing the client to spend minutes in PBKDF2 computation. Real
  PostgreSQL uses 4096; 600K matches the OWASP PBKDF2 ceiling.
- Add poll_flush after write_all completes. TLS streams (rustls, etc.)
  may buffer outgoing data and only transmit on explicit flush. Without
  this, pipelined messages could stall waiting for the TLS layer to
  flush, causing apparent hangs on authenticated connections.

SQLite (sqlite.rs):
- When a SqliteTransaction is dropped without commit/rollback, the
  existing code sets needs_rollback=true but relies on the *next*
  operation to drain it. If no subsequent operation arrives, the
  database stays locked indefinitely. Now explicitly spawns a blocking-
  pool task that acquires the connection lock and executes ROLLBACK
  immediately, preventing lock starvation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
